SAN LEANDRO HEALTHCARE CENTER

Address
368 JUANA AVENUE, SAN LEANDRO, CA 94577

Setting
Urban
4 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 4/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 4/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.53 3.80 4.61 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.36 0.66 0.36 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.26 0.84 1.28 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.91 2.29 2.96 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.62 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.10 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
4.20
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.27
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
4.27
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
45.3%
annual
RN turnover
50.0%
annual
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.35
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.98
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-11-07 12 10 2 52 1 52
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2022-11-17 11 10 1 72 1 72
Total weighted health score
57.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (27)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

3 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
24 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2025-11-26 2025-12-26
0553 Allow resident to participate in the development and implementation of his or her person-centered plan of care. D 2025-11-26 2025-12-26
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2024-11-07 2024-11-12
0640 Encode each resident’s assessment data and transmit these data to the State within 7 days of assessment. D 2024-11-07 2024-11-12
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2024-11-07 2024-11-12
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-11-07 2024-11-12
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2024-11-07 2024-11-12
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-11-07 2024-11-12
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2024-11-07 2024-11-12
0847 Inform resident or representatives choice to enter into binding arbitration agreement and right to refuse. E 2024-11-07 2024-11-12
0848 Provide a neutral and fair arbitration process and agree to arbitrator and venue. E 2024-11-07 2024-11-12
0912 Provide rooms that are at least 80 square feet per resident in multiple rooms and 100 square feet for single resident rooms. B 2024-11-07 2024-11-12
0624 Prepare residents for a safe transfer or discharge from the nursing home. D 2023-09-12 2023-09-28
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. E 2022-11-17 2022-12-20
0583 Keep residents' personal and medical records private and confidential. D 2022-11-17 2022-12-20
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. D 2022-11-17 2022-12-20
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2022-11-17 2022-12-20
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. E 2022-11-17 2022-12-20
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2022-11-17 2022-12-20
0800 Provide each resident with a nourishing, palatable, well-balanced diet that meets his or her daily nutritional and special dietary needs. F 2022-11-17 2022-12-20
0838 Conduct and document a facility-wide assessment to determine what resources are necessary to care for residents competently during both day-to-day operations (including nights and weekends) and emergencies. E 2022-11-17 2022-12-20
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2022-11-17 2022-12-20
0912 Provide rooms that are at least 80 square feet per resident in multiple rooms and 100 square feet for single resident rooms. B 2022-11-17 2022-12-20
0583 Keep residents' personal and medical records private and confidential. D 2019-03-28 2019-04-10
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2019-03-28 2019-04-10
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2019-03-28 2019-04-10
0912 Provide rooms that are at least 80 square feet per resident in multiple rooms and 100 square feet for single resident rooms. B 2019-03-28 2019-04-10
